character_dossiers.name: the captain
character_dossiers.biography: A weak, drunken man with a cutlass, frequently seen in the bar and parlour. He is described as physically declining, prone to fits of temper, and occasionally sings a sea-song.

character_dossiers.name: Black Dog
character_dossiers.biography: A figure seen by the narrator on the road, associated with the captain's crew. No further description is provided.

character_dossiers.name: the blind beggar
character_dossiers.biography: A blind, elderly man dressed in a tattered sea-cloak. He uses a stick and speaks in a sing-song tone, demanding assistance to find the captain.

character_dossiers.name: Doctor Livesey
character_dossiers.biography: A physician mentioned as being away from the house after the narrator's father's death. No further details are provided.

character_dossiers.name: my mother
character_dossiers.biography: The narrator's mother, who accompanies him in seeking help after the captain's death. She displays determination and resolve.

character_dossiers.name: Jim
character_dossiers.biography: The first-person narrator, who interacts with the blind beggar and the captain. Described as terrified and obedient to the blind man's commands.

character_dossiers.name: Mrs. Crossley
character_dossiers.biography: A local woman who provides a loaded pistol and promises assistance to the narrator and his mother.

character_dossiers.name: the doctor
character_dossiers.biography: A physician who was suddenly occupied with a case and did not return to the house after the narrator's father's death.
